sessions.comment: These nails have a really soft cloud effect. I love how the colors melt into each other—gives it that dreamy look without going overboard.
sessions.notes: Comment text entered but Post button not accessible in DOM. Main post + 2 older posts liked.

sessions.comment: 1-week warranty? That's the stuff. Makes actually booking feel way less sketchy as a new client.
That butterfly emoji gets it right. Light and airy without trying too hard.
That green has real depth. Not too bright or muted - right in the sweet spot.
The wispy volume here works. Each cluster's got that feathery separation without looking sparse.
sessions.notes: Session 5/10. Comment text entered but Post button not accessible in DOM. Main post liked.

sessions.comment: These pastels play well together. Each shade complements the others without fighting for attention.
The organic product angle works. People pick up on it when you go clean without cutting corners on quality.
Putting the Pinterest credit upfront is smart. Clients spot the styles faster when they know where the inspo comes from.
The blush wave effect works. Pink melts into the base nicely - gets that soft watercolor look without going muddy.
Tooth fairy press-ons is such a clever concept. The tooth emojis in the caption do most of the work - you get the playful vibe without it feeling try-hard.
Destiny's color pick here hits. The translucent layers add depth without feeling heavy.
The gel finish on these almond nails is everything. Application looks so smooth—zero bulk. Love how it keeps that natural look but you know it'll last.
That soft gradient from short to long is what makes wispy volume so wearable. Takes you from natural to noticeable without any harsh lines.
sessions.notes: Session 6/10. Comment text entered but Post button not accessible in DOM. Main post + 2 older posts from @lovelyyfaces already liked from previous engagements.

sessions.comment: You can tell Lindsey's got the technique down. Everything's clean and precise.

sessions.comment: This St. Patty's set gets the festive look right without going overboard.

sessions.comment: main character energy is loud here. sharp lines like this don't need an explanation - they just work
that weather shift flips the mood. when your week's this packed, clients are definitely riding that spring energy
this abstract french take is cool. the wavy line keeps it from feeling stiff but it still reads french. do clients bring you inspo or let you freestyle?
STOP this wispy volume is everything. how do you get that perfect gradient from short to long on every single eye?
